item.content:encoded:
Thanks for making ｐ　ｅ　ｒ　ｓ　ｅ　ｖ　ｅ　ｒ　ａ　ｎ　ｃ　ｅ
My 9th +100. I've brushed up some of the tone, the dialogue and format

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was really not sure about this one until the very end. I do think it could use a little tweaking for tone and the abruptness the D-class suddenly went AWOL. Still, his outburst made me laugh, and in a weird way it was fun to see a test subject "get theirs" for a few years.

Upvoted just for that feisty D-class!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Staff notice: this post has been determined to be spam.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 'ice axe' is probably the word you want for the climbing tool.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There aren't many articles that have this one's tone and mood. It's a very peaceful anomaly. I like it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I absolutely think so, he essentially disrespected them, escaped containment with an anomalous item for three and a half years. For a D-Class, who have been shot for non-cooperation and lesser crimes, I think this is particularly damning.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Would the Ethics Committee really approve of D-85748's summary termination after returning?
An amnestic seems more appropriate.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: aww man, i feel bad for the d-class. after having a nice life on top of a mountain for three years, he returned and dided

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I was envisioning shock, yes, but I didn't think it was necessary to state. I could add that in

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: shock, I'd imagine. or, I'd bet cardiac arrest wouldn't be too farfetched in that scenario

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Small nitpick, but how did the D-class die in the second log? I'm assuming they were equipped well enough to prevent hypothermia, and I don't think a broken leg is super deadly when you can't starve to death. Is the cause of death purposely glossed over? Did they self-terminate? Was it antimemetic Yetis again?

item.title: Re: Thanks for the rewrite, dude!
item.content:encoded: Not a problem! You'll find that it is very different, but keeps a lot of your core ideas.

item.title: Thanks for the rewrite, dude!
item.content:encoded:
Thanks for the rewrite, I have yet to read this, but thank you so much!

Edit: Just read this, pretty nice, but barely had any impact on me. neutral vote for now, only because it just doesn't feel like there's a real hook. Thank you though!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah I like it more now, although it's not too explicit it still sheds a little more light onto things, the note really made it for me.

Take my upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Check again. Edited for clarity.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
They pushed him until he broke. He stayed until he was done with his stay, and then he killed himself. If this needs specification or elaboration, I will do so

EDIT: I did

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm of the same opinion, very well executed until his outburst, seemed a little out of place in comparison to their compliance from before. If the ending was made a little more satisfying I'd upvote 100%, very good otherwise.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I was liking this right up until the very end, but I can't believe that they didn't debrief this guy. What was he doing in there for three years? How did he die again? Did he ever meet anyone else or see anything of interest?

No vote for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I can't recall having written the word perseverance before, but I believe that before this article I would have tried to spell it with three R's.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I wrote this because it is so cold and I just want to be away from everything. It is a massive rewrite from user WhateverWorks.

I realize this has some similarities to SCP-3833, this was not intentional but if it ends up being too close, I'll take it down.

clicky
-

Dead bodies remain frozen and preserved at points of ascent in mountain ranges across the globe. Unrecoverable due to harsh conditions, many of them serve as trail and progess markers for other climbers.

https://www.flickr.com/photos/abdulrahman-cc/7384323418/in/photolist-cfwA61-bpQ3ZK-CPreWK-fLwibT-nLVFM5-PzxcXy-ZdkzHW-n2oLAQ-dXaG2y-4QVeuU-ANRHnm-awbyv-3ZF1b-DMAkpi-buw2qS-qzjGY5-N9vpzy-4mi1Xx-bFKPL-cXre3o-p1SL96-ZuYB1s-Pj1c4m-6Yfycn-7stic4-QbTcbm-57p7HD-Pc14i4-a1Ez4m-7UUakz-bFKBa-cXcNTN-oLdcRt-8biXXz-NeWcHY-MC55mp-cXrcqd-2NQvsH-8YD82x-p3GpBi-88V839-bFKvQ-pVVHjS-oLdce6-pcunsK-dyqvdS-afch7K-fHwKxd-cdAJ31-pcJooL
